claim: LangSmith run types (LLM, retriever, tool, chain, prompt, parser) enable type-specific rendering and filtering of traces, improving debugging visibility
actionable: When tracing LLM applications, always specify run types to unlock LangSmith's filtering, comparison, and playground features for faster debugging
source_title: Getting Started with LangSmith (2/8): Types of Runs

claim: Document rendering in LangSmith requires specific format with page_content, type, and metadata keys to display readably instead of as JSON
actionable: When logging retriever runs to LangSmith, always format documents with the correct schema to avoid unreadable JSON output
source_title: Getting Started with LangSmith (2/8): Types of Runs

claim: LangSmith's playground feature becomes available only when run type is set to LLM, enabling in-browser prompt iteration on captured LLM calls
actionable: Use LangSmith playground for prompt engineering by ensuring your traced function uses run_type='llm' with correct input/output formats
source_title: Getting Started with LangSmith (2/8): Types of Runs
